pred_reason: SWC-105 applies due to unchecked call return values in transferFrom and WETH withdrawal enabling reentrancy and unauthorized transfers. SWC-107 covers timestamp dependency in approval functions which could be manipulated by miners. Evidence: TokenUtils.pullTokensIfNeeded uses transferFrom without origin validation (SWC-105); TokenUtils.withdrawWeth enables reentrancy via ETH callback (SWC-105); DFSRegistry uses block.timestamp for timing without safeguards (SWC-107)

SWC-101 (Integer Overflow/Underflow) is appropriate as the contract contains multiple unprotected arithmetic operations in critical functions. These vulnerabilities can lead to array corruption, incorrect token positioning, and potential contract bricking. Evidence: getTokens() function lacks overflow protection at line ~202; unregisterToken() has unprotected arithmetic at line ~161; registerTokenInternal() contains unsafe operations at line ~222

The audit process encountered a critical failure preventing standard vulnerability classification, as all AI models failed during report generation. This technical error necessitates the SWC-OTHER label since no code-specific findings could be produced. The absence of completed analysis means conventional SWC identifiers cannot be applied. Evidence: All AI models failed during report generation, with Deepseek_School API failing after 0.23s; No security findings could be generated due to complete system failure; Error occurred in Report Generator - FormatConverterNode, preventing audit completion

SWC-101 is applicable due to integer overflow/underflow vulnerabilities in transfer functions that enable token manipulation. SWC-107 applies because the approve function's race condition allows front-running attacks on allowance changes. Evidence: Integer overflow/underflow in transfer/transferFrom enables balance manipulation (SWC-101).; Approve function vulnerable to front-running, allowing dual allowance use (SWC-107).; Both issues confirmed in code via manual audit and external evidence references.

The SWC-101 label applies due to the integer underflow in SafeMath.ceil, which violates arithmetic safety. SWC-107 is appropriate because the reentrancy vulnerability in transferFrom breaks the checks-effects-interactions pattern. Evidence: Integer underflow occurs in SafeMath.ceil when subtracting 1 from overflowed values (Finding 2); transferFrom updates state after external calls via event emissions, enabling reentrancy attacks (Finding 1)
